candidate.title: Off-chain session token format with JWT-compatible envelope
candidate.description: Define a standardized off-chain session token that wraps a verified handshake result into a JWT-compatible envelope (RFC 7519). After on-chain verifyHandshake succeeds, the relayer issues a signed JWT whose claims include humanNullifier, agentNullifier, scopeCommitment, and sessionNonce — enabling stateless verification by downstream services without additional on-chain calls. The token MUST include an `iss` claim bound to the relayer's DID, a `nbf`/`exp` window, and a `bolyra_proof_tx` claim linking back to the on-chain settlement. This bridges the gap between Bolyra's on-chain verification and the HTTP-native auth flows that web services expect.

candidate.title: Machine-readable conformance test vectors with deterministic fixtures
candidate.description: Create a conformance/ directory with 30+ JSON test vectors organized by circuit (HumanUniqueness, AgentPolicy, Delegation) and scenario (valid, expired-credential, revoked-human, stale-root, scope-superset-rejection, cumulative-bit-violation, nonce-replay). Each vector includes deterministic private inputs, expected public outputs, and a 'should_pass' boolean. Use a fixed test secret/keypair so any implementation can reproduce identical commitments and nullifiers. This is the minimum bar for a credible multi-implementation ecosystem and should be referenced normatively from the IETF draft.

candidate.title: Register application/bolyra-proof+cbor media type and define proof envelope
candidate.description: The protocol currently has no standardized wire format for transmitting proofs between prover and verifier. Define a CBOR-based proof envelope (application/bolyra-proof+cbor) with a fixed schema: version (uint), circuit_id (text), proving_system (text: 'groth16'|'plonk'), public_signals (array<bstr>), proof (bstr), and optional delegation_chain (array<envelope>). Include a CDDL grammar in the IETF draft. This replaces ad-hoc JSON serialization in the SDK and enables language-agnostic interop — any conformant implementation can parse and forward proofs without SDK-specific knowledge.

candidate.title: Complete DID resolution algorithm with revocation and delegation discovery
candidate.description: The existing did-method-bolyra.md spec defines the DID syntax but lacks a complete resolution algorithm. Specify the full CRUD lifecycle: Create (enrollment tx → DID Document with verificationMethod), Read (resolve commitment → on-chain lookup of enrollment event + current Merkle inclusion status), Update (not applicable — commitments are immutable), Deactivate (revocation nullifier check). Add a `delegationService` endpoint in the DID Document that points to the delegation chain discovery API. Include concrete JSON-LD examples for each operation and error codes for stale-root and revoked-identity resolution failures.

candidate.title: Structured error codes with recovery hints
candidate.description: SDK currently throws generic Error('proof verification failed'). Introduce a BolyraError class with machine-readable codes (STALE_ROOT, EXPIRED_CREDENTIAL, SCOPE_MISMATCH, NONCE_REUSED, NULLIFIER_SPENT) and a `.hint` field with the specific fix ('Root is 3 blocks behind; re-fetch with registry.latestRoot()'). Map on-chain revert reasons from IdentityRegistry's custom errors to SDK-level diagnostics. This is the single biggest DX gap — developers can't debug failures without reading circuit code.

candidate.title: LangChain credential middleware with automatic scope-gated tool access
candidate.description: Build a LangChain CallbackHandler + BaseTool wrapper that intercepts tool invocations and requires a valid Bolyra AgentPolicy proof before execution. The middleware maps LangChain tool names to permission bitmask bits (e.g., SQLDatabaseTool → READ_DATA, StripeChargeTool → FINANCIAL_SMALL) via a declarative YAML config. On first call, it runs proveHandshake() and caches the proof for the session nonce lifetime. Publish as @bolyra/langchain on npm and bolyra-langchain on PyPI, with a 5-line quickstart that wraps an existing AgentExecutor.

candidate.title: Standardized proof envelope with application/bolyra+json content type
candidate.description: Define a JSON envelope format for proof payloads that all framework integrations use: {version, circuit, publicSignals[], proof{pi_a, pi_b, pi_c}, metadata{framework, sdkVersion, provingTimeMs}}. Register application/bolyra+json as the content type. This lets any HTTP-based agent framework (MCP servers, OpenAI function calling proxies, REST tool servers) accept Bolyra proofs via a standard Authorization header (Bearer bolyra:<base64-envelope>) without framework-specific code. Include a generic Express/FastAPI middleware that validates the header, so tool servers get ZK auth in 3 lines.

candidate.title: Off-chain JWT session token from verified handshake for framework middleware
candidate.description: After a successful on-chain verifyHandshake(), the SDK should emit a signed JWT containing the nullifierHash, scopeCommitment, and sessionNonce with a configurable TTL (default 5 min). Framework middleware (LangChain, CrewAI, AutoGen) can then verify this JWT locally for subsequent tool calls in the same session without re-proving on-chain. This eliminates the biggest adoption blocker: multi-second proof generation on every tool invocation. The JWT issuer key is derived from the agent's operator EdDSA key, so verification stays self-sovereign.
